comments.text: Let $\phi_t:=\limsup_{n\to\infty} f(n;t)\big/\binom{n}{t-1}$. Pikhurko and Verstraëte [PiVe09] improved the earlier upper bounds by proving\[ 1 \leq \phi_t \le \min\!\left\{\frac{7}{4},\,1+\frac{2}{\sqrt{t}}\right\}\quad\text{for all }t\ge 3, \]so in particular $\phi_t\to 1$ as $t\to\infty$. Moreover, for $t=3$ they obtained the uniform bound\[ f(n;3)\le \frac{13}{9}\binom{n}{2}\quad\text{for all }n. \]Reference. [PiVe09] Pikhurko, Oleg, and Jacques Verstraëte. "The maximum size of hypergraphs without generalized 4-cycles." Journal of Combinatorial Theory, Series A 116.3 (2009): 637-649. (The site has been updated to address this comment.)

The first sentence in the description under this problem needs a correction: the value of $f(n;2)$ should read as follows. In the graph case $t=2$, one has \(f(n;2)=\left(\tfrac12+o(1)\right)n^{3/2}\). (Also, the sentence should read “for $t=2$” rather than “for $n=2$”.) (The site has been updated to address this comment.)
